metadata.description: FishTrack Species Knowledge Center — Full biological profiles for all BFAR MIMAROPA Marinduque fish species.
metadata.citation: Republic of the Philippines, Department of Agriculture, Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic Resources (BFAR) Regional Fisheries Office - MIMAROPA, Provincial Fishery Office, Boac, Marinduque. Noted by: Joel G. Malabana, OIC, PFO.
metadata.note: Keys are 'scientific_name|common_name'. Add new profiles here — no code changes needed. 'default_profile' is used as a fallback for unmapped species.

default_profile.feeding: Omnivorous, feeding on zooplankton and algae
default_profile.importance: Supports small-scale coastal artisanal fisheries and local food markets.

Decapterus macrosoma|Swordfish.habitat: Demersal and pelagic coastal waters, schooling near the surface at night
Decapterus macrosoma|Swordfish.feeding: Feeds primarily on small pelagic zooplankton, copepods, and fish larvae
Decapterus macrosoma|Swordfish.breeding: March to May during seasonal transitions; spawns in offshore aggregations
Decapterus macrosoma|Swordfish.importance: Essential commercial forage fish locally, widely consumed fresh and dried.
Decapterus macrosoma|Swordfish.fishing_method: Round haul seine (salambaw), purse seine, lift net

Lampris guttatus|Bullet Tuna.habitat: Open ocean (epipelagic), reef slopes, congregates around FADs and floating objects
Lampris guttatus|Bullet Tuna.feeding: Carnivorous; feeds on small squids, crustaceans, and larval fishes
Lampris guttatus|Bullet Tuna.breeding: Spawn in warm offshore waters during summer (March–July); high fecundity
Lampris guttatus|Bullet Tuna.importance: High commercial value for local canning and fresh fish markets; key pelagic predator.
Lampris guttatus|Bullet Tuna.fishing_method: Handline, trolling, purse seine, FAD fishing

Carangoides oblongus|Frigate Tuna (Tulin.habitat: Coastal and offshore pelagic; schooling species often following prey aggregations
Carangoides oblongus|Frigate Tuna (Tulin.feeding: Carnivorous; feeds on small fish, squid, and crustaceans
Carangoides oblongus|Frigate Tuna (Tulin.breeding: Year-round spawning with peak activity in warm months (April–August)
Carangoides oblongus|Frigate Tuna (Tulin.importance: Supports small-scale coastal artisanal fisheries and local food markets. Popular in regional wet markets.

Stolephorus spp.|Yellowfin Tuna (Tambako.habitat: Epipelagic, coastal and oceanic waters; often associated with thermocline layers
Stolephorus spp.|Yellowfin Tuna (Tambako.feeding: Feeds on small fish, squid, and zooplankton; opportunistic apex predator
Stolephorus spp.|Yellowfin Tuna (Tambako.breeding: Year-round spawning with peaks during dry season (March–May); spawns offshore
Stolephorus spp.|Yellowfin Tuna (Tambako.importance: Major target for commercial purse seiners and local handline fishers; fetches high market value.
Stolephorus spp.|Yellowfin Tuna (Tambako.fishing_method: Handline, troll line, purse seine, FAD fishing

Katsuwonus pelamis|Indian Mackerel (Bang.habitat: Highly migratory, epipelagic schooling species in tropical and subtropical seas
Katsuwonus pelamis|Indian Mackerel (Bang.feeding: Active predator; feeds on fish, squid, crustaceans, and cephalopods
Katsuwonus pelamis|Indian Mackerel (Bang.breeding: Year-round spawning in tropical waters; peak during warmer months
Katsuwonus pelamis|Indian Mackerel (Bang.importance: Globally one of the most important commercial tuna species; heavily canned and traded.

Coryphaena hippurus|Round Scad (Galunggo.habitat: Open coastal waters, schooling near surface in warm, clear water
Coryphaena hippurus|Round Scad (Galunggo.feeding: Planktivorous; filters copepods, larval crabs, and small crustaceans
Coryphaena hippurus|Round Scad (Galunggo.breeding: Year-round spawning with dry season peaks; highly prolific
Coryphaena hippurus|Round Scad (Galunggo.importance: The 'national fish' of affordable protein in the Philippines; critical commercial value for low-income households.
Coryphaena hippurus|Round Scad (Galunggo.fishing_method: Round haul seine, gill net, purse seine

Rastrelliger brachysoma|Moon Fish (Opah).habitat: Mesopelagic to epipelagic open ocean; solitary and slow-moving
Rastrelliger brachysoma|Moon Fish (Opah).feeding: Feeds on squid, small fish, crustaceans, and jellyfish
Rastrelliger brachysoma|Moon Fish (Opah).breeding: Spawning biology poorly known; thought to breed offshore in tropical waters
Rastrelliger brachysoma|Moon Fish (Opah).importance: Rare bycatch of commercial longliners; prized as a novelty fish in specialty restaurants.

Caesio cuning|Oblong Trevally.habitat: Coastal waters, reef edges, estuaries; often near FADs
Caesio cuning|Oblong Trevally.feeding: Piscivorous; pursues smaller schooling fish and invertebrates
Caesio cuning|Oblong Trevally.breeding: March to June; spawns near reef structures
Caesio cuning|Oblong Trevally.importance: Commercially important species in coastal Philippine fisheries; sold fresh in markets.

Scomberomorus commerson|Anchovies (Dilis.habitat: Shallow sandy bays, coastal zones, estuaries; forms dense surface schools
Scomberomorus commerson|Anchovies (Dilis.feeding: Filters zooplankton, copepods, and microalgae; filter feeder
Scomberomorus commerson|Anchovies (Dilis.breeding: Year-round spawning, highly prolific; batch spawner
Scomberomorus commerson|Anchovies (Dilis.importance: Massive ecological significance as a primary food source for larger commercial fish; sold dried (tuyo) or as fish sauce (patis).
Scomberomorus commerson|Anchovies (Dilis.fishing_method: Bag net, fine-mesh trawl, beach seine

Scomberomorus commerson|Skipjack Tuna (G.habitat: Pelagic, schooling in open ocean; associated with thermoclines and FADs
Scomberomorus commerson|Skipjack Tuna (G.feeding: Active predator feeding on small fish, crustaceans, and cephalopods
Scomberomorus commerson|Skipjack Tuna (G.breeding: Spawns year-round in tropical waters; highly fecund
Scomberomorus commerson|Skipjack Tuna (G.importance: Highly important commercial species globally and locally; heavily traded in markets and canned.
Scomberomorus commerson|Skipjack Tuna (G.fishing_method: Pole-and-line, purse seine, FAD fishing, handline

Sardinella lemuru|Dolphin Fish (Mahi-mah.habitat: Open ocean epipelagic; common around floating debris and FADs
Sardinella lemuru|Dolphin Fish (Mahi-mah.feeding: Opportunistic predator; feeds on fish, squid, and crustaceans
Sardinella lemuru|Dolphin Fish (Mahi-mah.breeding: March–May peak season; fast-growing and early maturing
Sardinella lemuru|Dolphin Fish (Mahi-mah.importance: Highly prized game and export fish; popular in restaurants and seafood markets.

Sardinella gibbosa|Short Mackerel (Hasah.habitat: Inshore coastal waters, bays, estuaries; schooling near surface
Sardinella gibbosa|Short Mackerel (Hasah.feeding: Filter feeds on phytoplankton and zooplankton
Sardinella gibbosa|Short Mackerel (Hasah.breeding: Spawns multiple times during rainy season; batch spawner
Sardinella gibbosa|Short Mackerel (Hasah.importance: Key food source for local coastal communities; sold fresh, dried, or salted.
Sardinella gibbosa|Short Mackerel (Hasah.fishing_method: Ring net, gill net, beach seine

Lutjanus spp.|Fusilier (Dalagang Bukid).habitat: Coral reefs and reef slopes; forms large mid-water schools
Lutjanus spp.|Fusilier (Dalagang Bukid).feeding: Feeds on zooplankton, small crustaceans, and fish larvae in the water column
Lutjanus spp.|Fusilier (Dalagang Bukid).breeding: March to May; spawns in aggregations near reef drop-offs
Lutjanus spp.|Fusilier (Dalagang Bukid).importance: Commercially important schooling fish; significant component of Philippine reef fisheries catch.

Epinephelus spp.|Spanish Mackerel (Tanig.habitat: Coastal waters, coral reefs, rocky structures; solitary ambush predator
Epinephelus spp.|Spanish Mackerel (Tanig.feeding: Piscivorous; preys on sardines, anchovies, and squids near reefs
Epinephelus spp.|Spanish Mackerel (Tanig.breeding: March to July in coastal reef systems; some species are protogynous hermaphrodites
Epinephelus spp.|Spanish Mackerel (Tanig.importance: Prized game and food fish; fetches premium prices in local and national seafood restaurants.

Lates calcarifer|Narrow-Barred Spanish M.habitat: Coastal marine waters, estuaries; highly migratory species
Lates calcarifer|Narrow-Barred Spanish M.feeding: Piscivorous apex predator; feeds on fish, squid, and crustaceans
Lates calcarifer|Narrow-Barred Spanish M.breeding: March to July; spawns in coastal reef and channel areas
Lates calcarifer|Narrow-Barred Spanish M.importance: One of the most commercially important mackerel species in Southeast Asian fisheries.

Tylosurus crocodilus|Bali Sardinella.habitat: Coastal bays, inshore shelf waters; forms large schools near surface
Tylosurus crocodilus|Bali Sardinella.feeding: Filter feeds on phytoplankton, zooplankton, and organic particles
Tylosurus crocodilus|Bali Sardinella.breeding: Multiple spawning events year-round; peak in rainy season
Tylosurus crocodilus|Bali Sardinella.importance: Major sardine species important for canning industry and coastal food security.
Tylosurus crocodilus|Bali Sardinella.fishing_method: Ring net, purse seine, beach seine

Family Exocoetidae|Goldstripe Sardinella.habitat: Coastal and offshore surface waters; schooling species
Family Exocoetidae|Goldstripe Sardinella.feeding: Filter feeds on phytoplankton and zooplankton
Family Exocoetidae|Goldstripe Sardinella.breeding: March to May spawning peak; forms dense spawning aggregations
Family Exocoetidae|Goldstripe Sardinella.importance: Important commercially; sold fresh and dried in coastal markets.

Selar crumenophthalmus|Snapper (Maya-may.habitat: Coral reefs, rocky sea bottoms, artificial reefs; solitary or small groups
Selar crumenophthalmus|Snapper (Maya-may.feeding: Benthic predator; feeds on crabs, shrimps, mollusks, and small fish
Selar crumenophthalmus|Snapper (Maya-may.breeding: Spawns during spring and summer warming cycles; uses reef aggregations
Selar crumenophthalmus|Snapper (Maya-may.importance: Premium market fish; high eco-tourism value for divers and fishers.
Selar crumenophthalmus|Snapper (Maya-may.fishing_method: Handline, gill net, spearfishing, fish traps

Family Scaridae|Grouper (Lapu-lapu).habitat: Coral reefs and rocky crevasses; ambush predator hiding in reef structures
Family Scaridae|Grouper (Lapu-lapu).feeding: Ambush predator; eats crabs, lobsters, and small fishes
Family Scaridae|Grouper (Lapu-lapu).breeding: Year-round spawning around new moon periods; protogynous hermaphrodite
Family Scaridae|Grouper (Lapu-lapu).importance: Extremely high value live food fish trade; critical for maintaining reef food-web health.
Family Scaridae|Grouper (Lapu-lapu).fishing_method: Handline, fish traps, spearfishing, live fish trading

Decapterus kurroides|Emperor Fish (Apaha.habitat: Sandy and muddy bottom coastal and reef habitats; often near estuaries
Decapterus kurroides|Emperor Fish (Apaha.feeding: Benthic carnivore; feeds on crustaceans, mollusks, worms, and small fish
Decapterus kurroides|Emperor Fish (Apaha.breeding: Year-round; spawning peaks during rainy season near estuaries
Decapterus kurroides|Emperor Fish (Apaha.importance: Prized food and sport fish; common in Philippine markets and restaurants.
Decapterus kurroides|Emperor Fish (Apaha.fishing_method: Handline, longline, gill net, fish traps

Elagatis bipinnulata|Needlefish (Agujon).habitat: Surface waters, coastal areas, estuaries; solitary or small schools
Elagatis bipinnulata|Needlefish (Agujon).feeding: Carnivorous surface predator; feeds on small fish and crustaceans
Elagatis bipinnulata|Needlefish (Agujon).breeding: Spawns near coastal structures and floating vegetation
Elagatis bipinnulata|Needlefish (Agujon).importance: Minor commercial importance; caught incidentally by coastal fishers.
Elagatis bipinnulata|Needlefish (Agujon).fishing_method: Cast net, gill net, torch fishing (night)

Aluterus monoceros|Flying Fish (Ikan Ter.habitat: Open ocean surface; glides above water to escape predators
Aluterus monoceros|Flying Fish (Ikan Ter.feeding: Feeds on phytoplankton, algae, and small invertebrates near surface
Aluterus monoceros|Flying Fish (Ikan Ter.breeding: March–May; attaches eggs to floating seaweed and debris
Aluterus monoceros|Flying Fish (Ikan Ter.importance: Minor commercial importance; used as bait and dried fish in coastal areas.
Aluterus monoceros|Flying Fish (Ikan Ter.fishing_method: Dip net, light fishing (pangulong ilaw), cast net

Nemipterus spp.|Bigeye Scad (Matangbaka).habitat: Coastal shelf waters; schooling species in mid-water column
Nemipterus spp.|Bigeye Scad (Matangbaka).feeding: Opportunistic feeder on zooplankton, small fish, and invertebrates
Nemipterus spp.|Bigeye Scad (Matangbaka).breeding: Year-round spawning; peaks during monsoon transitions
Nemipterus spp.|Bigeye Scad (Matangbaka).importance: Highly abundant; one of the most important commercial small pelagics in Marinduque.
Nemipterus spp.|Bigeye Scad (Matangbaka).fishing_method: Round haul seine, gill net, purse seine

Genus Naso|Parrot Fish (Loro).habitat: Coral reefs; grazes on coral polyps and reef algae
Genus Naso|Parrot Fish (Loro).feeding: Herbivorous; uses beak-like teeth to scrape algae and coral
Genus Naso|Parrot Fish (Loro).breeding: Year-round; forms large spawning aggregations at reef edges
Genus Naso|Parrot Fish (Loro).importance: Important reef ecosystem species; sand production and reef health maintenance.

Genus Siganus|Redtail Scad.habitat: Open coastal and offshore surface waters; forms large schools
Genus Siganus|Redtail Scad.feeding: Planktivorous feeder; feeds on zooplankton and small crustaceans
Genus Siganus|Redtail Scad.importance: Important commercial small pelagic; significant catch volume in Marinduque.

Cubiceps whiteleggii|Rainbow Runner trou.habitat: Open ocean pelagic; often associated with FADs and floating objects
Cubiceps whiteleggii|Rainbow Runner trou.feeding: Carnivorous; feeds on fish, squid, and crustaceans
Cubiceps whiteleggii|Rainbow Runner trou.breeding: March–May; spawns offshore in warm open waters
Cubiceps whiteleggii|Rainbow Runner trou.importance: Popular game fish with good commercial value; prized for sport fishing.

Family Mullidae|Unicorn Leatherjacket.habitat: Reef habitats and rocky outcrops; often found singly or in pairs
Family Mullidae|Unicorn Leatherjacket.feeding: Herbivorous to omnivorous; feeds on algae, small invertebrates, and detritus
Family Mullidae|Unicorn Leatherjacket.breeding: March–June; spawns near rocky reef structures
Family Mullidae|Unicorn Leatherjacket.importance: Minor commercial importance; caught as bycatch and sold locally.

Family Balistidae|Threadfin Bream (Bisug.habitat: Sandy to muddy soft bottoms; demersal schooling species
Family Balistidae|Threadfin Bream (Bisug.feeding: Feeds on benthic invertebrates, worms, and crustaceans
Family Balistidae|Threadfin Bream (Bisug.breeding: March–June; demersal egg layer on sandy bottoms
Family Balistidae|Threadfin Bream (Bisug.importance: Important commercial demersal species; widely consumed fresh or as fish paste (bagoong).

Sphyraena barracuda|Unicorn Fish.habitat: Coral reefs and rocky areas; grazes on coral and rocky substrate
Sphyraena barracuda|Unicorn Fish.feeding: Herbivorous; feeds on algae and small invertebrates on reef substrate
Sphyraena barracuda|Unicorn Fish.breeding: Year-round; spawning aggregations near reef crests
Sphyraena barracuda|Unicorn Fish.importance: Reef ecosystem keystone species; important for reef health through grazing.

Selar crumenophthalmus|Siganids (Rabbitf.habitat: Seagrass beds, reef flats, and coastal mangrove margins
Selar crumenophthalmus|Siganids (Rabbitf.feeding: Herbivorous; grazes on algae, seagrass, and detritus
Selar crumenophthalmus|Siganids (Rabbitf.breeding: Year-round; spawns in large aggregations during full moon at reef edges
Selar crumenophthalmus|Siganids (Rabbitf.importance: Very important in local coastal fisheries; high demand in fresh fish markets.
Selar crumenophthalmus|Siganids (Rabbitf.fishing_method: Gill net, fish corrals, beach seine

Trichiurus lepturus|Shadow Driftfish.habitat: Mesopelagic open ocean; found in deep water columns and around drifting objects
Trichiurus lepturus|Shadow Driftfish.feeding: Feeds on zooplankton, small crustaceans, and gelatinous prey
Trichiurus lepturus|Shadow Driftfish.breeding: Poorly documented; thought to spawn in deep offshore waters
Trichiurus lepturus|Shadow Driftfish.importance: Rare bycatch in deep-sea fisheries; occasional incidental capture by longliners.

Mugil cephalus|Goatfish (Bangus).habitat: Shallow coastal waters, brackish estuaries, lagoons, tidal flats
Mugil cephalus|Goatfish (Bangus).feeding: Feeds on bottom detritus, algae, diatoms, and small benthic organisms
Mugil cephalus|Goatfish (Bangus).breeding: Spawns offshore during cooler winter months (November–January)
Mugil cephalus|Goatfish (Bangus).importance: Major aquaculture species in the Philippines; critical local commodity for milkfish (bangus) farms.
Mugil cephalus|Goatfish (Bangus).fishing_method: Fish pens (fishpond), gill net, beach seine

Chanos chanos|Triggerfish.habitat: Coral reefs, rocky outcrops, and rubble zones
Chanos chanos|Triggerfish.feeding: Omnivorous; feeds on algae, coral polyps, invertebrates, and small fish
Chanos chanos|Triggerfish.breeding: Year-round; lays sticky demersal eggs in reef crevices
Chanos chanos|Triggerfish.importance: Minor commercial importance; incidentally caught by reef fishers.

Oreochromis niloticus|Barracuda.habitat: Open coastal water, reef edges, and passes; fast open-water predator
Oreochromis niloticus|Barracuda.feeding: Apex piscivorous predator; feeds on fish, squid, and large crustaceans
Oreochromis niloticus|Barracuda.breeding: Spawns offshore during warmer months; pelagic spawner
Oreochromis niloticus|Barracuda.importance: Moderate commercial value; popular game fish for sport anglers.

Decapterus macrosoma|Red Bigeye Scad.habitat: Mid-water column over sandy and rocky reefs; schooling species
Decapterus macrosoma|Red Bigeye Scad.feeding: Feeds on zooplankton, copepods, and small invertebrates
Decapterus macrosoma|Red Bigeye Scad.importance: Important commercial scad species in Philippine inshore fisheries.

Lampris guttatus|Largehead Hairtail.habitat: Mesopelagic to demersal; found over muddy and sandy bottoms in deeper shelf areas
Lampris guttatus|Largehead Hairtail.feeding: Carnivorous; feeds on fish, squid, and crustaceans with ambush strikes
Lampris guttatus|Largehead Hairtail.breeding: March–May; spawns in deep coastal shelf waters
Lampris guttatus|Largehead Hairtail.importance: Commercially important demersal species in Southeast Asia; popular in local wet markets.

Carangoides oblongus|Mullet (Bubuli).habitat: Coastal inshore waters, brackish bays, estuaries, and tidal channels
Carangoides oblongus|Mullet (Bubuli).feeding: Omnivorous; feeds on algae, organic detritus, and small invertebrates
Carangoides oblongus|Mullet (Bubuli).breeding: Offshore spawning during cooler months; juveniles migrate to brackish waters
Carangoides oblongus|Mullet (Bubuli).importance: Important food fish in coastal communities; cultivated in brackish-water ponds.
Carangoides oblongus|Mullet (Bubuli).fishing_method: Cast net, gill net, fish corrals, fish pens

Stolephorus spp.|Milkfish (Bangus).habitat: Coastal marine, brackish estuaries, seagrass beds, mangrove areas
Stolephorus spp.|Milkfish (Bangus).feeding: Herbivorous-omnivorous; feeds on algae, detritus, phytoplankton
Stolephorus spp.|Milkfish (Bangus).breeding: Offshore spawning in marine waters; adults migrate from sea to brackish ponds
Stolephorus spp.|Milkfish (Bangus).importance: National fish of the Philippines; cornerstone of aquaculture industry and household nutrition.
Stolephorus spp.|Milkfish (Bangus).fishing_method: Fish pens (bangus farming), gill net, beach seine

Katsuwonus pelamis|Tilapia.habitat: Freshwater lakes, rivers, ponds, brackish lagoons; highly adaptable
Katsuwonus pelamis|Tilapia.feeding: Omnivorous; feeds on algae, aquatic plants, detritus, and small insects
Katsuwonus pelamis|Tilapia.breeding: Year-round mouthbreeder; highly reproductive — multiple spawning cycles per year
Katsuwonus pelamis|Tilapia.importance: Most popular aquaculture species in the Philippines; vital for inland food security and aquaculture livelihoods.
Katsuwonus pelamis|Tilapia.fishing_method: Fish pens, cage culture, gill net, hook-and-line
